title: Space Jam (1996) original promotional website
why: A 1996 site still served unchanged; a live reference specimen of early-web design patterns.
license: proprietary content; cataloged as metadata only

title: The Devil's Dictionary — Ambrose Bierce (Project Gutenberg #972)
why: Public-domain satirical glossary with a rigid entry structure; could be parsed into a reusable structured dataset.

title: NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day API
why: Free public API with an archive back to 1995; the deep archive is rarely explored programmatically.
license: US government work, generally public domain

title: The case of the 500-mile email
why: Classic sysadmin folklore; exemplar of a genre of small technical narratives with no index or catalog.

title: The Met Open Access dataset + Collection API (CC0)
why: CC0 metadata for 470k+ artworks and ~406k public-domain images, live JSON API with no auth; huge, legally clean, and rarely explored beyond thumbnails.

title: Library of Congress loc.gov JSON API
why: No-key JSON API over books, maps, photos, newspapers, archived web; underused relative to its breadth, and pairs with LoC free-to-use sets.
license: US government; content free to use per LoC data-exploration terms

title: Smithsonian Open Access (edan) metadata repository
why: Millions of CC0 records across 19 museums, bulk JSON on GitHub/AWS plus API via api.data.gov; a cross-institution complement to the Met set.
